title: BBC News - Breaking news, video and the latest top stories from the U.S. and around the world
meta_description: Visit BBC News for the latest news, breaking news, video, audio and analysis. BBC News provides trusted World, U.S. and U.K. news as well as local and regional perspectives. Also entertainment, climate, business, science, technology and health news.
text_content: News LIVE Trump says US will ‘obliterate’ Iran’s power plants if Strait of Hormuz not open before 48-hour deadline Tehran warns it will retaliate against US-linked energy infrastructure in the Middle East if its power plants are attacked. Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground. 10 hrs ago Middle East 'Peace is a gradual thing': How land, cattle and identity fuel a deadly Nigerian conflict A lack of justice and trust in the security forces has perpetuated deadly violence in Plateau state. 10 hrs ago Africa The Bachelorette's messy break-up with its unlikely star Taylor Frankie Paul The TikTok and reality TV star was a risky choice for the squeaky clean franchise - and ended up being a wrong one. 12 hrs ago US & Canada Pop megastars BTS electrify historic centre of Seoul with comeback concert The world's biggest band returns after more than three years with a huge concert in the South Korean capital. 13 hrs ago Asia Did Australia's under-16s social media ban work? Three months since it came into effect, teenagers tell the BBC how the world-first law has impacted their lives so far. 16 hrs ago Australia National blackout hits Cuba for second time in a week Another total failure of its power grid underlines Cuba's hardship under a US-imposed fuel blockade. 9 hrs ago Latin America Italy is voting on whether to change its constitution. What does this mean for Meloni? The dry subject matter of this weekend's vote seems obscure but for many it is a vote on the PM. 11 hrs ago Europe Robert Mueller, ex-FBI chief who led Trump-Russia investigation, dies at 81 The fight to control the narrative in the Afghan-Pakistan conflict The forgotten Indian woman trailblazer in British medicine Trump makes Pearl Harbor remark in meeting with Japan's PM Iran war Iranian missiles injure 160 in towns near Israeli nuclear site Israel is investigating how ballistic missiles got through the country's sophisticated air defences. British foreign secretary denounces 'reckless Iran threats' after missiles fired at US-UK base Iran reportedly fired two ballistic missiles at Indian Ocean base, but neither reached the target. Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last? Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February. More to explore AI videos of sexualised black women removed from TikTok after BBC investigation Dozens of Instagram and TikTok accounts have used AI avatars to promote explicit content, the BBC finds. 10 hrs ago Technology BTS Arirang review: K-pop idols rekindle their fire The band's 10th album sees them return to the rebellious, rap-heavy sound of their early days. 1 day ago Culture US hints at easing Iran oil curbs - what it means for India India was a major buyer of Iranian crude, peaking at about 11.5% of its total purchase, before 2018 sanctions tightened. 2 days ago World Sombr stopped a show over safety - but are concerts in the UK really that risky? Musicians are increasingly interrupting their own shows to help fans - but venues might be safer than you think. 11 hrs ago Culture 'Club vibes without the hangover': The twenty-somethings going out - in the gym Young people are driving a gym boom as more fitness spaces are transformed into vibrant hangouts. 10 hrs ago Business Did Australia's under-16s social media ban work? Three months since it came into effect, teenagers tell the BBC how the world-first law has impacted their lives so far. 16 hrs ago Australia Quebec's religious-symbols ban that some women say treats them as outsiders heads to top court Canada's Supreme Court will hear a challenge to Quebec's Bill 21, which bars some public sector workers, including teachers, from wearing religious symbols. 11 hrs ago US & Canada Most watched 1 Did Australia's under-16s social media ban work? 2 BBC in Cuba as island plunged into darkness 3 Watch: Real possum spotted on toy animal shelf at Australian airport 4 Why are Iranian footballers claiming asylum in Australia returning to Iran? 5 Israeli strike next to British journalist is not AI-generated Also in news Trump threatens to send ICE into airports unless funding deal reached The president said ICE would "do security" as airport security staff have gone without pay for weeks due to a partial government shutdown. 14 hrs ago US & Canada Watch: BBC in Cuba as island plunged into darkness More than 10 million people are enduring power cuts after Cuba's national electrical grid collapsed again. 2 hrs ago World Bodycam footage of Justin Timberlake's 2024 drink-driving arrest released The pop star's legal team had tried to prevent the release of footage from his arrest, but reached an agreement. 16 hrs ago US & Canada Pentagon restrictions on press violate First Amendment, judge rules The Department of Defense had required reporters to agree to certain rules on what information they could gather in order to maintain access to the Pentagon. 19 hrs ago US & Canada Socialists battle to hold Paris as France holds mayoral elections The capital could shift to the right after 25 years under the Socialists if Rachida Dati defeats Emmanuel Grégoire. 4 hrs ago Europe Thousands evacuated as Hawaii faces worst flooding in 20 years "Don't take this storm lightly," Hawaii Governor Josh Green warned on Saturday, as more rain is expected on Oahu and Maui. 13 hrs ago US & Canada Russian drone attack kills two in Ukraine ahead of talks in US, officials say Two children, aged 11 and 15, were also injured in the attack which took place in Zaporizhzhia. 15 hrs ago Europe Most read 1 Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran 2 Italy is voting on whether to change its constitution. What does this mean for Meloni? 3 AI videos of sexualised black women removed from TikTok after BBC investigation 4 Iranian missiles injure 160 in towns near Israeli nuclear site 5 The Bachelorette's messy break-up with its unlikely star Taylor Frankie Paul 6 National blackout hits Cuba for second time in a week 7 Campervan parking ban set to be made permanent 8 Homeless sleeping pods used for 71 nights 9 Foreign secretary denounces 'reckless Iran threats' after missiles fired at Diego Garcia 10 Pop megastars BTS electrify historic centre of Seoul with comeback concert Sport LIVE Premier League: Build-up to Tyne-Wear derby in first of three games Follow live coverage of three Premier League games on Sunday, starting with the Tyne-Wear derby as Newcastle host rivals Sunderland. Who needs cup final victory most - Arteta or Guardiola? Premier League leaders Arsenal face title rivals Manchester City in the EFL Cup final on Sunday, but how important could the result prove in title race? 5 hrs ago EFL Cup The race no-one wants to win - are Everton serious top-five contenders? The fight for Champions League football appears to be turning into one no-one wants to win, but have Everton snuck into the race? 11 hrs ago Premier League From crutches to world crown - Kerr thanks family after indoor 3,000m triumph Josh Kerr completes his post-injury return to the top of a global podium in thrilling fashion as he reclaims his world indoor 3,000m title. 15 hrs ago Athletics Outrun and in decline - are Rosenior's Chelsea near crisis point? Chelsea enter freefall after another defeat - 3-0 away at Everton - and have two weeks over the international break to stew on a damaging 10 days. 12 hrs ago Chelsea 'If Liverpool play like that at PSG it could be 10-0' Arne Slot needs answers quickly or Liverpool's unwanted records may no longer be his only worry. 17 hrs ago Liverpool 'Heartbreaking' - fighter pay debate laid bare at UFC London The debate around fighter pay reemerges at UFC London amid some career-defining performances on the undercard. 4 hrs ago MMA
